nvt.name: OpenSSL Heap Buffer Overflow Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
nvt.tags: summary=The remote host is running a version of OpenSSL that is affected by a heap buffer overflow.|insight=An attacker can craft a malicious TLS handshake that overflows an internal buffer, leading to memory corruption.|impact=Successful exploitation allows remote, unauthenticated code execution in the context of the affected service.|affected=OpenSSL versions prior to 3.0.14 and 1.1.1x.|vuldetect=Checks the version of OpenSSL reported by the TLS service.
nvt.solution: Update OpenSSL to version 3.0.14 or later and restart all dependent services.

result.description: Installed version: OpenSSL 3.0.2 Fixed version: OpenSSL 3.0.14 Detection method: TLS banner inspection on port 443/tcp.

nvt.name: TLS/SSL Server Supports Deprecated TLSv1.0 / TLSv1.1 Protocols
nvt.tags: summary=The remote service accepts connections using deprecated TLS protocol versions.|impact=Weak protocol versions are subject to downgrade and cryptographic attacks such as BEAST and POODLE.|affected=Services negotiating TLSv1.0 or TLSv1.1.|vuldetect=Attempts TLS handshakes using each protocol version.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Disable TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 and require TLSv1.2 or TLSv1.3.

result.description: The service on 443/tcp negotiated TLSv1.0 during the assessment.

nvt.name: OpenSSH Weak Key Exchange Algorithms Supported
nvt.tags: summary=The remote SSH server is configured to allow weak key exchange algorithms.|impact=Weak key exchange may permit cryptographic attacks against the confidentiality of the session.|affected=OpenSSH servers offering diffie-hellman-group1-sha1.|vuldetect=Reviews the algorithms advertised during the SSH handshake.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Restrict KexAlgorithms in sshd_config to strong, modern algorithms.

result.description: The SSH server on 22/tcp offers: diffie-hellman-group1-sha1.

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags: summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows uptime computation.|impact=A remote attacker may compute the host uptime.|affected=Hosts with RFC1323 TCP timestamps enabled.|vuldetect=Sends a TCP SYN with the timestamp option set.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Disable TCP timestamps where operationally acceptable.

result.description: The host responded with TCP timestamps enabled.

nvt.name: Traceroute / Hop Count
nvt.tags: summary=Informational traceroute results for the target host.|vuldetect=Performs a traceroute to the target.
